title: Fermented Glassy-Jelly Egyptian Beef Tagine
description: A post-workout Egyptian dish featuring minced beef, confit-tomato, and fermented elements in a glassy-jelly sauce, friendly for ayurvedic rajasic diets. Best served with warm pita bread or couscous for a filling meal.

ingredients.name: minced beef
ingredients.notes: Choose grass-fed beef for optimal health benefits

ingredients.name: confit tomato
ingredients.notes: Homemade or store-bought, low-sodium for a better fermentation process

ingredients.name: ginger
ingredients.notes: Fresh or dried, depending on your preference

ingredients.name: galangal
ingredients.notes: Optional but recommended for its unique flavor

ingredients.name: cumin powder
ingredients.notes: Use fresh cumin for better digestion

ingredients.name: fenugreek seeds
ingredients.notes: Ayurvedic-friendly, high in protein and fiber

ingredients.name: fermented milk
ingredients.notes: Use homemade or store-bought fermented milk for optimal probiotics

ingredients.name: ajwain
ingredients.notes: Also known as carom seeds, adds a unique flavor

ingredients.name: salt
ingredients.notes: Himalayan pink salt for better mineral absorption

ingredients.name: black pepper
ingredients.notes: Freshly ground for a better flavor

ingredients.name: glassy-jelly agar agar
ingredients.notes: Buy glassy-jelly agar agar powder online or in local health food stores

ingredients.name: water
ingredients.notes: Use filtered water for better fermentation

instructions: Combine minced beef, confit tomato, ginger, galangal, cumin powder, coriander powder, fenugreek seeds, and salt in a large bowl, mixing well for optimal flavors.
Transfer the mixture to a clay or ceramic tagine and let it marinate for at least 2 hours or overnight for better flavor absorption.
Heat the oil in a pan over medium heat, add the mixture and cook for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally for a smooth consistency.
Add the fermented milk, ajwain, and glassy-jelly agar agar to the mixture and stir well for a glassy-jelly texture.
Reduce the heat to low and let it simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the glassy-jelly sauce thickens.
Serve warm with pita bread or couscous for a filling meal.
Garnish with chopped cilantro or parsley for a fresh look and flavor.
Adjust the seasoning with black pepper and salt for better taste.
Let the tagine rest for 10-15 minutes before serving for a better texture and flavor absorption.
Store leftovers in the refrigerator for up to 3 days or freeze for up to 2 months for future meals.
Reheat the leftovers with a small amount of water or broth to restore the glassy-jelly texture.
Experiment with different spices and flavor combinations to make this recipe your own!
